forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRC1C93CD73746F4C0Q2PP86
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a480
+title: Switch frontend perspective tab bar filter to view_id with kind fallback
+---
+## What
+
+Make the formula-bar perspective tabs show **only** the perspectives scoped to the currently active view (by id), with a documented fallback so legacy kind-scoped perspectives still appear. Newly created perspectives must be saved with the active view's id so they don't leak to sibling views of the same kind.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — replace the kind-only filter in `usePerspectiveTabBar` (currently around lines 164–168):
+  ```ts
+  const viewKind = activeView?.kind ?? "board";
+  const filteredPerspectives = useMemo(
+    () => perspectives.filter((p) => p.view === viewKind),
+    [perspectives, viewKind],
+  );
+  ```
+  becomes:
+  ```ts
+  const activeViewId = activeView?.id;
+  const activeViewKind = activeView?.kind ?? "board";
+  const filteredPerspectives = useMemo(
+    () =>
+      perspectives.filter((p) => {
+        if (p.view_id != null) return p.view_id === activeViewId;
+        return p.view === activeViewKind; // legacy shared-by-kind
+      }),
+    [perspectives, activeViewId, activeViewKind],
+  );
+  ```
+- Any call site that creates a new perspective from this component (e.g. "New perspective" action / command — search via `code_context grep code "add.*perspective"` scoped to `kanban-app/ui/src`) must pass `view_id: activeView.id` alongside `view: activeView.kind` so new perspectives are scoped from the moment they're created. If the creation path goes through a backend command, ensure the command call includes the `view_id` argument.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` — already extended by the prerequisite task; ensure the field is being used (no further change expected unless the prerequisite task did not land it).
+
+### Behavior
+
+- Two grid-kind views with different ids show **different** perspective tab sets in the formula bar.
+- Existing perspectives loaded from disk that lack `view_id` continue to appear across every view of their kind — the legacy compat rule from the data-model task is honored on the client too.
+- New perspectives created from a specific view are pinned to that view's id and do not appear when switching to a sibling view of the same kind.
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Backend filter changes — covered by "Switch backend perspective filters to view_id with kind fallback" (this task's prerequisite via the data-shape task; the backend task can land in parallel with this one).
+- Migrating existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` files — see "Migrate existing perspective YAMLs to carry view_id where unambiguous".
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` filters by `view_id` when set, and falls back to `view` (kind) otherwise.
+- [ ] New perspective creation paths pass `view_id` derived from the currently active view's id.
+- [ ] Switching between two `kind: "grid"` views with different ids shows two distinct perspective tab sets in the formula bar — verified by a regression test.
+- [ ] Existing perspectives without `view_id` still appear in every view whose kind matches — verified by a regression test.
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test perspective-tab-bar` is green.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New spatial/regression test: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.view-id-scoping.test.tsx`:
+  - Mount `<PerspectiveTabBar>` inside the existing test harness that provides `usePerspectives` + `useViews`.
+  - Fixtures: two views with `kind: "grid"`, ids `view-a` and `view-b`; two perspectives — perspective P1 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: "view-a"` and perspective P2 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: "view-b"`.
+  - Set active view to `view-a` → assert P1 tab is rendered, P2 is NOT.
+  - Set active view to `view-b` → assert P2 tab is rendered, P1 is NOT.
+- [ ] Same test file, additional case: a legacy perspective P3 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id` absent → appears for BOTH `view-a` and `view-b`, but NOT for the board view.
+- [ ] Update any existing `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` fixture that asserts on perspective visibility — if fixtures relied on kind-only matching for grid views, refactor them to either set `view_id` explicitly or assert legacy fallback behavior so the intent is captured.
+- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test perspective-tab-bar` — green.
+- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` (full UI suite) — green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the two-grid regression test first, watch it fail (today both grids share perspectives), then swap the filter and the test creation path.
+- Look up how `usePerspectives` and `useViews` are wired in the test harness — there is an existing pattern in `perspective-tab-bar.enter-rename.spatial.test.tsx` and `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx`. Match it; do not invent a new mount helper.
+- Do NOT introduce manual smoke-test steps. The regression must be fully automated through the existing UI test harness.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What

Make the formula-bar perspective tabs show **only** the perspectives scoped to the currently active view (by id), with a documented fallback so legacy kind-scoped perspectives still appear. Newly created perspectives must be saved with the active view's id so they don't leak to sibling views of the same kind.

### Files to modify

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — replace the kind-only filter in `usePerspectiveTabBar` (currently around lines 164–168):
  ```ts
  const viewKind = activeView?.kind ?? "board";
  const filteredPerspectives = useMemo(
    () => perspectives.filter((p) => p.view === viewKind),
    [perspectives, viewKind],
  );
  ```
  becomes:
  ```ts
  const activeViewId = activeView?.id;
  const activeViewKind = activeView?.kind ?? "board";
  const filteredPerspectives = useMemo(
    () =>
      perspectives.filter((p) => {
        if (p.view_id != null) return p.view_id === activeViewId;
        return p.view === activeViewKind; // legacy shared-by-kind
      }),
    [perspectives, activeViewId, activeViewKind],
  );
  ```
- Any call site that creates a new perspective from this component (e.g. "New perspective" action / command — search via `code_context grep code "add.*perspective"` scoped to `kanban-app/ui/src`) must pass `view_id: activeView.id` alongside `view: activeView.kind` so new perspectives are scoped from the moment they're created. If the creation path goes through a backend command, ensure the command call includes the `view_id` argument.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/types/kanban.ts` — already extended by the prerequisite task; ensure the field is being used (no further change expected unless the prerequisite task did not land it).

### Behavior

- Two grid-kind views with different ids show **different** perspective tab sets in the formula bar.
- Existing perspectives loaded from disk that lack `view_id` continue to appear across every view of their kind — the legacy compat rule from the data-model task is honored on the client too.
- New perspectives created from a specific view are pinned to that view's id and do not appear when switching to a sibling view of the same kind.

### Out of scope

- Backend filter changes — covered by "Switch backend perspective filters to view_id with kind fallback" (this task's prerequisite via the data-shape task; the backend task can land in parallel with this one).
- Migrating existing `.kanban/perspectives/*.yaml` files — see "Migrate existing perspective YAMLs to carry view_id where unambiguous".

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` filters by `view_id` when set, and falls back to `view` (kind) otherwise.
- [ ] New perspective creation paths pass `view_id` derived from the currently active view's id.
- [ ] Switching between two `kind: "grid"` views with different ids shows two distinct perspective tab sets in the formula bar — verified by a regression test.
- [ ] Existing perspectives without `view_id` still appear in every view whose kind matches — verified by a regression test.
- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test perspective-tab-bar` is green.

## Tests

- [ ] New spatial/regression test: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.view-id-scoping.test.tsx`:
  - Mount `<PerspectiveTabBar>` inside the existing test harness that provides `usePerspectives` + `useViews`.
  - Fixtures: two views with `kind: "grid"`, ids `view-a` and `view-b`; two perspectives — perspective P1 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: "view-a"` and perspective P2 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id: "view-b"`.
  - Set active view to `view-a` → assert P1 tab is rendered, P2 is NOT.
  - Set active view to `view-b` → assert P2 tab is rendered, P1 is NOT.
- [ ] Same test file, additional case: a legacy perspective P3 with `view: "grid"`, `view_id` absent → appears for BOTH `view-a` and `view-b`, but NOT for the board view.
- [ ] Update any existing `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` fixture that asserts on perspective visibility — if fixtures relied on kind-only matching for grid views, refactor them to either set `view_id` explicitly or assert legacy fallback behavior so the intent is captured.
- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test perspective-tab-bar` — green.
- [ ] Run: `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` (full UI suite) — green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the two-grid regression test first, watch it fail (today both grids share perspectives), then swap the filter and the test creation path.
- Look up how `usePerspectives` and `useViews` are wired in the test harness — there is an existing pattern in `perspective-tab-bar.enter-rename.spatial.test.tsx` and `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx`. Match it; do not invent a new mount helper.
- Do NOT introduce manual smoke-test steps. The regression must be fully automated through the existing UI test harness.
Switch frontend perspective tab bar filter to view_id with kind fallback

 Make the formula-bar perspective tabs show **only** the perspectives scoped to the currently active view (by id), with a documented fallback so legacy kind-scoped perspectives still appear. Newly created perspectives must be saved with the active view's id so they don't leak to sibling views of the same kind.
